objective: Create Demo Checkpoint 1 PR4 as the optional server-side avatar/video provider integration boundary after merged PR #238 / issue #237 and post-PR238 reconciliation PR #240 / issue #239. Positive claims: C1-VIDEO-001 server-side avatar/video provider adapter keeps mock/local as default for local/dev/test/CI; C1-VIDEO-002 optional real avatar/video providers remain disabled unless provider config, env-only key, selected-provider source facts, quota reservation/refund, timeout, retry, duplicate-spend, output validation, retention/deletion, and redaction gates pass; C1-VIDEO-003 media artifacts are bound to source run ID, trace ID, language, audience, script checksum, citation refs, evaluation ID/status/checksum, TTS/audio checksum when present, provider ID/model/version, provider job ID, artifact checksum, disclosure text/version, and retention/deletion state; C1-VIDEO-004 provider responses, URLs, downloaded bytes, scripts, transcripts, TTS metadata, and generated video metadata are treated as untrusted and validated before storage, display, download, or logging; C1-VIDEO-005 provider-stock, synthetic, prompt-generated, or otherwise non-identifiable identity assets are the Checkpoint 1 default and cloned identity remains out of scope; C1-VIDEO-006 every changed PR4 claim maps to an executable test, gate, official source fact, human-only checklist row, or documented non-goal. Negative invariants: no hosted-demo access system, invite flow, public URL, deployment, demo polish, cloned voice, cloned face/avatar, digital twin, replica-profile creation, Product Mode 2, public synthetic-media distribution, production-readiness claim, provider account setup, dashboard configuration, paid plan activation, wallet funding, paid spend, real provider calls in CI, or real provider test calls without a fresh PR4 plan and explicit written human-owner approval.

forbidden: real provider test calls without explicit written owner approval
real avatar or video generation without explicit written owner approval
